title: He made Tetris — and earned $0 for over a decade #Shorts
desc: Alexey Pajitnov created Tetris in the Soviet Union in the mid-1980s, and it became one of the most played games in history — its Game Boy version alone sold about 35 million copies. But because the Soviet state controlled the rights, Pajitnov earned no royalties for over a decade. Only in 1996, after co-founding The Tetris Company, did the man who built Tetris finally get paid.
